company.name: Lumen Collective
company.ceo_priority_q4: Execution velocity — every team must show measurable weekly progress against top 1-2 priorities

employees.name: Adam Ankeny
kpis: Onboard 25 new creators/week to the platform
employees.writing_style: Concise, numbers-forward. Leads with metrics, short sentences. Consistent format daily.
employees.hidden_truth: HIGH PERFORMER. Hits or exceeds targets every day. Control group — should rank #1.

employees.name: Avery Holmseth
kpis: Drive 15% expansion revenue from existing book
employees.writing_style: Long narrative paragraphs. Buries metrics in prose. Optimistic tone ('great call,' 'positive energy,' 'feeling good'). Avoids numbers and dates.
employees.hidden_truth: OPTIMISM-REALITY GAP. Updates sound great but metrics are slipping. Northwind Athletics renewal stalled (4 days 'almost there'). Petalcrest expressed concerns but Avery downplays it.

employees.name: Sean Cretti
employees.writing_style: Dry, table-like, numbers-forward. ROAS, CPM, CTR everywhere. No fluff.
employees.hidden_truth: SUBMISSION GAP. Skips Tuesday and Wednesday entirely — does not post any update on those two days. Posts on Monday, Thursday, and Friday only. Thursday and Friday updates cover what happened on the skipped days. Numbers are fine but cadence violation is the issue. You MUST only generate 3 updates (Monday, Thursday, Friday) — do NOT generate Tuesday or Wednesday updates.

employees.name: Jeff Collard
employees.writing_style: Short, breezy, casual. One-liners. 'Crushed it today, 45 dials 💪'. Sometimes forgets to mention meetings booked.
employees.hidden_truth: VANITY METRICS GAP. Hits dial count every day (activity metric) but meetings-booked is quietly declining: 5 → 4 → 3 → 2 → 2. Never draws attention to it.

employees.name: Hannah Kargman
kpis: Ship Creator Matching v2 by end of quarter
employees.writing_style: Thoughtful, qualitative. 'Alignment,' 'blockers,' 'stakeholder input,' 'iterating.' Rarely commits to dates. Talks process more than output.
employees.hidden_truth: NO FORWARD MOTION + MISSED KPI. Same blocker (aligning with eng on matching algorithm) mentioned Mon-Fri with no escalation or progress. User research KPI: only 2 completed sessions total (Mon, Tue). Wed-Fri: rescheduling, silence, no recovery to 5.

updates.text: 28 creators onboarded today, already past the 25/wk target. Response time at 18hr avg. Northwind campaign crew fully ramped, no issues. Tomorrow's batch of 32 apps is prepped and ready to go
31 new creators today. Response time 16hr. Petalcrest creator pool at 94% utilization. Had one QC flag but resolved same-day. Wednesday queue ready — 29 in final review
27 creators onboarded. 17hr response time. Harborline Foods campaign launched with full crew, 12 creators, zero delays. Caught a batch data error in pending apps, fixed same-day no impact
29 creators added. 19hr response time. Week total 115+. Ridgeway Outdoors needed expedited onboarding for seasonal cohort — got 8 creators done in 12hr. Signature backlog cleared

26 creators today, 141 for the week (5.6x target). Avg response time 17.8hr across the board. All campaigns staffed — Northwind, Petalcrest, Harborline, Ridgeway. Cinderhouse Coffee cohort queued for Monday. Zero escalations, clean week

Really great call with Northwind today, super positive energy on the renewal. They love the creative and we're almost locked on extending another year. Petalcrest had a good sync too, strong interest in expanding Q1 budget. Feeling good about both of these

Northwind still in a great place, another productive conversation. They're reviewing contracts internally, should have something by end of week. Petalcrest mentioned some budget concerns but I'm confident we can work through it. Overall momentum feels strong, really positive about the quarter

Northwind progressing, just in internal approvals which is normal. Expecting to hear back Friday. Petalcrest brought up pricing concerns but felt like a negotiation tactic, they're still interested. Had a few other positive calls with smaller accounts. Renewals tracking well

Northwind still in legal review, honestly a good sign that they're taking it seriously. They said it's almost there. Petalcrest feels like standard back and forth on terms. Had another encouraging call about their expansion plans. Really optimistic about how these are shaping up

Northwind still pending legal, they keep saying early next week. Frustrating but normal enterprise rhythm. Petalcrest called today and tbh the tone shifted — they're pausing expansion for "budget realignment" which is new. Still figuring out what changed. Pipeline is solid overall but keeping a close eye on these two, they're our biggest renewal plays

Cinderhouse ROAS 3.2x (target 2.8x), CPM down 8% WoW. Launched 2 experiments — Petalcrest carousel ads at 1.8% CTR early, Harborline video reels looking strong on engagement. CAC down 4% vs last week. One more experiment pending creative review

catching up on tue/wed — Petalcrest carousel hit 2.1% CTR, Harborline reels at 12% VTR. Launched third experiment wed, Ridgeway static ads audience test. CPM dropped another 3% on Cinderhouse. Portfolio ROAS 3.2x, CAC down 7% WTD. All three experiments performing

week close — 3 experiments total. Cinderhouse ROAS 3.2x, CPM down 11% WoW, CAC down 7%. Petalcrest carousel best at 2.4% CTR. Harborline reels stable 11% VTR. Ridgeway still in learning phase but early signals positive. Portfolio exceeded 2.8x target. Ready for scaling next week
45 dials today, 5 meetings booked. Northwind referral path converting really well. solid start to the week
48 dials, 4 meetings — Petalcrest, Harborline, Ridgeway, plus a net-new. locked in
46 dials, 3 meetings booked. had a reschedule but filled the slot quick. staying on the big accounts
44 dials, 2 meetings. some no-shows on callbacks but the ones who pick up are converting. still on pace
47 dials, 230+ for the week (crushed 200 target). strong outreach velocity all week. keeping momentum into next week

Matching v2 — still aligning with eng on algorithm requirements, speed vs accuracy tradeoff. They're reviewing infra constraints. Ran first user research session this morning with a creator cohort, really valuable feedback. Got another session tomorrow

Another sync with eng on the matching algorithm, they want more clarity on weighting before committing sprint capacity. Iterating on requirements, should have stakeholder input by Wednesday. Ran second research session with a brand client, good insights on creator filtering

Still aligning with eng on matching algo. They flagged our requirements might be overspecified so going back to prioritization with leadership. Scope shifting a bit. Had to reschedule this week's research session to next week, priorities shifted

Still in alignment phase on Matching v2 architecture. Eng has concerns about current proposal so doing another round of stakeholder input. Research sessions pushed to next week, had to prioritize internal alignment. Should have clearer direction by Friday

Spent the week on matching algorithm alignment, eng and product still iterating. Getting closer but slower than I'd like. Research is behind — only got 2 sessions done this week out of 5 target. Ramping up next week to catch up. Will get clarity on timeline next week
